hooks.name: Ship work on session end
hooks.description: Automatically commits, pushes, opens a PR, and asks permission to merge when a session ends

action.prompt:
Before closing this session, perform the following ship workflow:

1. CHECK FOR CHANGES: Run `git status` to see if there are uncommitted changes. If there are none, skip the rest.

2. COMMIT with best-practice Conventional Commits format:
   - Use the format: `<type>(<scope>): <short summary>` (max 72 chars for subject)
   - Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore, ci, perf, style
   - Scope should reflect the affected module (e.g., pipeline, infra, api, db)
   - Body should explain WHAT changed and WHY (not HOW)
   - If multiple logical changes exist, create separate commits for each
   - Stage specific files per commit (do NOT use `git add .` for mixed changes)

3. PUSH to a feature branch:
   - If already on a feature branch, push there
   - If on main/master, create a branch named `feat/<short-description>` or `fix/<short-description>` and push
   - Use `git push -u origin <branch>`

4. OPEN A PULL REQUEST using `gh pr create` with best-practice documentation:
   - Title: same as the primary commit subject line (max 72 chars, imperative mood)
   - Body MUST follow this structure:
     ## Summary
     Brief description of what this PR does and why.

     ## Changes
     - Bullet list of key changes, grouped by area

     ## Testing
     - What was tested (unit tests, integration, manual verification)
     - Commands run and their outcomes

     ## Checklist
     - [ ] Code follows project conventions
     - [ ] Tests pass locally
     - [ ] No secrets or credentials committed
     - [ ] Documentation updated if needed

   - Do NOT auto-merge

5. WAIT FOR CI: After pushing, monitor CI checks:
   - Run `gh pr checks <PR-number> --watch` to wait for CI workflows (lint, pipeline-ci, db-ci, infra-ci, security, coverage, docker)
   - If any check fails, report which check failed and the error. Attempt to fix the issue, commit the fix, and push again. Repeat up to 2 times.
   - Do NOT proceed to merge if CI is failing.

6. ASK PERMISSION: Once CI passes, show the user:
   - The PR URL
   - A summary of CI check results (all green / any warnings)
   - Ask: "All CI checks pass. PR is ready. Would you like me to merge it (squash), or would you prefer to review first?"
   - Only merge if the user explicitly confirms
   - Use `gh pr merge --squash` when approved

If any step fails, report the error and stop — do not force-push or skip checks.
